metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4907
metadata.comment:
Rationale: The study shows the existence of a synaptically localized ATG-9 pool that undergoes exo-endocytosis and localizes at a subset of synaptic vesicles in C. elegans. The exo-endocytosis of ATG-9 is important for synaptic authopagy (Fig 5M). Additionally, in a mutant where Atg9 is abnormally accumulated due to an impaired exo-endocytosis, leads to decreased synaptic transmission.

Authors also show that ATG9A localizes at presynapses in mammalian synapses (Fig 6a-c, g-i; Fig 7b-c).. Experimental description: From M&M: "C. elegans CRISPR Transgenics
To introduce the lesion in unc-26/synaptojanin 1 associated with early-onset Parkinsonism (EOP), we used CRISPR-Cas9 to substitute CGA with CAA, and generate the homozygous mutation R216Q. CRISPR transgenic strain was generated by precision genome editing method using CRISPR-Cas9 and linear repair templates, as previously described (Paix et al., 2017) and using the targeted gene crRNA (GGCACUCGAUUCAACGUAC) and repair template ssODN (GACGTGTTGCTCTAATATCTCGTCTAAGTTGTGAGCGTGTCGGCACTCGATTCAACGTACAAGGAGCCAATTATCTCGGAAATGTGGCTAATTTCGTCGAGACTGAGCAATTGTTGCTTTT)"
